evidence.organoid_type: Protocols to generate region-specific cerebral organoids, including forebrain, midbrain, cerebellar, hippocampal, and hypothalamic organoids, are now established
evidence.organoid_type.infection_context: In both 3D cerebral organoid models, infection with Toxoplasma tachyzoites, of both Me49 and RH strains, lead to bradyzoite differentiation and formation of mature cysts, with cysts persisting up to 4 weeks post-infection, within the cerebral organoids
evidence.source_cells.cell_type: Human somatic cells exposed to Yamanaka factors, OCT4, KLKF4, SOX2 and c-MYC (OKSM), are reprogrammed into iPSCs which can be differentiated into different cell lineages
evidence.source_cells.cell_line: Human neurons were derived from NCRM-1 cells, a neural stem cell (NSC) line obtained from NIH, using a similar protocol as outlined in Figure 1A
evidence.matrix: null/not_reported — review paper describes no Matrigel or matrix embedding protocol
evidence.base_media: null/not_reported — review paper does not name a base medium
evidence.signaling_factors.BDNF_GDNF_cAMP: In brief, hiPSC can be neutralized via dual SMAD signaling inhibition producing neural stem cell (NSCs), followed by addition of specific growth factors such as BDNF and GDNF and cAMP to induce differentiation into postmitotic neurons
evidence.signaling_factors.concentrations: null/not_reported — no numerical doses given in review text for BDNF, GDNF, or cAMP
evidence.neural_induction.dual_SMAD: hiPSC can be neutralized via dual SMAD signaling inhibition producing neural stem cell (NSCs)
evidence.reprogramming_factors.OKSM: Human somatic cells exposed to Yamanaka factors, OCT4, KLKF4, SOX2 and c-MYC (OKSM), are reprogrammed into iPSCs which can be differentiated into different cell lineages
evidence.reprogramming_factors.BAM_NEUROD1: Human fibroblasts can be converted into iNs via a combination of 3 transcription factors, BRN2, ACSL1, and MYTL1, the BAM reprogramming factors, in addition to the neurodifferentiation factor, NEUROD1
evidence.differentiation_timeline.NSC_to_neuron: Differentiation into postmitotic neurons from NSCs typically takes 3-4 weeks.
evidence.differentiation_timeline.Table1: 4-6 months from fibroblasts; 3-6 weeks from iPSCs; 1-4 weeks from NSCs
evidence.cell_sources.Table1: Fibroblasts, keratinocytes, dental pulp cells, blood cells, renal epithelial cells
evidence.infection_model.2D.bradyzoite_onset: tachyzoite to bradyzoite stage conversion beginning within the first 12 hrs. post infection (p.i.) and mature cysts generated by 96h p.i.
evidence.infection_model.2D.persistence: Mature cysts developed near the neuronal soma and in the dendritic processes and could be maintained in neurons for up to 14 days p.i.
evidence.infection_model.3D.mature_cysts: cerebral organoids can be infected with tachyzoites which convert to bradyzoites and generate mature cysts by 5 days p.i.
evidence.infection_model.3D.persistence: cysts persisting in the outer layer of cerebral organoids, where mature neurons and some glial cells are found
evidence.infection_model.3D.duration: cysts to persist for at least 4 weeks post-infection

silver_precision_verified:signaling_factor.GDNF — confirmed in full text, no dose available
silver_precision_verified:signaling_factor.cAMP — confirmed in full text, no dose available

notes:
SOURCE TYPE: This is a REVIEW paper (Halonen 2023, Frontiers in Cellular and Infection Microbiology, doi:10.3389/fcimb.2023.1129451). It summarizes neurodifferentiation strategies for hiPSC/iN cell models used in Toxoplasma gondii infection research. It does not describe a single original protocol with precise media recipes; rather, it synthesizes published methods from the field.

SILVER PRECISION (11/11 = 100%): All 11 silver-extracted fields were verified correct against the full text. Key verified facts: organoid_type='cerebral' (confirmed), cell_type='iPSC' (confirmed), signaling factors BDNF/GDNF/cAMP (confirmed, mentioned by name only — no numerical doses exist in the review text). null values for matrix, base_media, and all concentrations were justified: the review text does not describe a named base medium, does not mention Matrigel or any matrix, and provides no ng/mL or μM values for any factor.

RECALL MISSES (9 fields): The silver extractor missed the following, all verified in full text and now captured above: (1) NCRM-1 NSC cell line from NIH; (2) dual SMAD inhibition as neural induction method; (3) Yamanaka/OKSM reprogramming factors OCT4/KLF4/SOX2/c-MYC; (4) BAM+NEUROD1 iN direct-conversion transcription factors BRN2/ASCL1/MYT1L; (5) NSC-to-postmitotic neuron timeline 3-4 weeks; (6) Table 1 timelines (iPSC 3-6 wk, NSC 1-4 wk, fibroblast 4-6 months); (7) Table 1 cell sources (fibroblasts, keratinocytes, dental pulp, blood, renal epithelial); (8) 2D cyst timeline (bradyzoite onset 12h, mature cysts 96h, persistence 14 days); (9) 3D cerebral organoid cyst timeline (mature cysts 5 days p.i., persistence ≥4 weeks, outer layer localization).

TYPOS IN SOURCE: The review contains apparent typos: 'KLKF4' for KLF4, 'ACSL1' for ASCL1, and 'MYTL1' for MYT1L. Verbatim quotes preserve the typos; normalized gene symbols are used in the structured data fields.

SCOPE NOTE: This paper is primarily an infection biology review. The 'organoid protocol' fields (signaling_factors, matrix, base_media) reflect only what the review text explicitly states about differentiation methods. For full protocol details, primary method papers (e.g., the Lancaster 2013 cerebral organoid protocol cited in the paper) would need to be consulted.
